name: Tushita Heaven
description: Tushita Heaven, also known as Tusita Heaven, is the fourth of the six heavens of the desire realm (Kāmadhātu) in Buddhist cosmology. It is considered a pure land and a place of great joy and contentment, residing above the Yama Heaven. Tushita is particularly significant as the current abode of the Bodhisattva Maitreya, the future Buddha, who awaits the opportune time to be reborn on Earth and guide sentient beings to enlightenment. It is a realm where beings with accumulated merit can be reborn to further their spiritual development, surrounded by divine beings and enjoying blissful existence. Tushita Heaven is described as a beautiful and radiant place, filled with gardens, palaces, and celestial beings who dedicate their time to studying and practicing the Dharma under Maitreya's guidance. Rebirth in Tushita is considered a highly auspicious event, offering a favorable environment for progressing towards Buddhahood. The celestial beings of Tushita are said to possess great wisdom and compassion, and they eagerly await Maitreya's descent to Earth to assist him in his mission of universal salvation. It is a place of refuge, learning, and preparation for the final stages of the path to enlightenment. The duration of life in Tushita is considerably long compared to human lifetimes, allowing beings ample time to cultivate virtue and advance spiritually. This heaven serves as a crucial staging ground for future Buddhas and a beacon of hope for all beings striving for liberation.
subtitle: Abode of Maitreya, Future Buddha

keyMyths.title: Maitreya's Residence
keyMyths.description: Tushita Heaven is where Maitreya Bodhisattva resides, awaiting his time to be reborn as the next Buddha. He teaches the Dharma to the Devas and Bodhisattvas residing there.

keyMyths.title: Rebirth in Tushita
keyMyths.description: Those who have accumulated sufficient merit can be reborn in Tushita Heaven, where they can continue their spiritual practice under Maitreya's guidance. This rebirth is considered highly auspicious.

sources.author: Rhys Davids, T.W.
sources.description: Provides context on early Buddhist cosmology.
sources.text: Provides context on early Buddhist cosmology.
